evals.prompt: Audit this React signup form for WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility issues. Use the wcag-audit skill format and severity rubric.
evals.expected_output: Findings in markdown-report format covering: (1) email input has no accessible label, flagged as failure citing 1.3.1 or 3.3.2; (2) password strength indicator uses color alone, flagged as failure citing 1.4.1; (3) submit button missing focus-visible style or note that focus indication cannot be statically verified, citing 2.4.7; (4) color contrast cannot be verified statically when colors come from CSS variables or Tailwind utilities, listed under 'Cannot be verified statically'; (5) output uses the failure/warning/info severity rubric.

evals.prompt: Run an accessibility audit on this Python data processing module: src/etl/transformers.py
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that no frontend surface is present and the audit does not apply. Does not produce a generic accessibility checklist for non-UI code. Either declines or asks whether the user meant to audit a different file.

evals.prompt: Review this navigation component against WCAG 2.2. Pay attention to new 2.2 criteria.
evals.expected_output: References at least one criterion new in WCAG 2.2 (2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured Minimum, 2.5.7 Dragging Movements, 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum, 3.2.6 Consistent Help, 3.3.7 Redundant Entry, or 3.3.8 Accessible Authentication Minimum). Distinguishes Level AA from Level A and AAA. Does not invent severity levels outside the failure/warning/info rubric.
